key_points: Morgan State added 300 beds after record enrollment surge
Housing demand outpacing supply at growing HBCUs generally
No specific DSU housing complaints found in data
key_quotes: Morgan may have more students than NCAT soon. This housing is needed.
It's cool the students get to live downtown.

key_points: Football atmosphere praised; DeSean Jackson revived DSU Hornets program
Greek life dominates HBCU social scenes, non-D9 students can feel excluded
Campus events draw non-students, creating safety concerns
key_quotes: The Delaware St. Hornets has been a welcome mat for The MEAC for years. DeSean Jackson has really turned that program around.
I don't like how much HBCUs center fraternities and sororities — what about SGA, the newspaper, the debate team?
Let the kids have fun. There's nothing wrong with this.

key_points: No direct DSU dining reviews found in available data
Jackson State's $21.7M dining expansion shows HBCUs investing in food
Dining quality at HBCUs varies widely; check campus-specific reviews
key_quotes: Jackson State University begins $21.7 million dining hall expansion — that is going to be beautiful!
No DSU-specific dining feedback available in this dataset.

key_points: NCCU student died after targeted harassment over not being D9
Homophobia reported as real stressor at some HBCUs
HBCU community culture praised for cognitive and emotional long-term benefits
key_quotes: NCCU student Zyarre Prince dies from targeted anonymous harassment — recounts feeling excluded due to homophobia and not being D9.
Spending your formative years in an environment that nurtures you and promotes your emotional and intellectual growth surely pays dividends.
Homophobia, okay, I understand the toll that could take. Not being D9? I don't get that.

key_points: DSU student posted urgent plea about tuition deadline with no options
Federal HBCU grant cuts of $350M create real financial pressure
Smaller HBCUs often offer better aid packages than Howard or Spelman
key_quotes: PLEASE HELP – My tuition deadline is coming up and I'm out of options.
Howard offered me 20k out of their 58k cost of attendance. Spelman offered me $0. Tuskegee offered me a full ride.
Getting somewhere sometimes is easier than staying — cost may be a factor in low graduation rates.

key_points: Only 7 of ~107 HBCUs have graduation rates above 50%
DSU subreddit is nearly inactive; almost no academic discussion exists
FAMU academic rankings rising; HBCU nursing programs posting elite results
key_quotes: Only 7 out of 107-ish HBCUs graduating more than half their students — the national average is 64%.
FAMU's academic programs ascend in national rankings, bolstering university's stature.
We get more done with less, IYKYK.

key_points: DSU administration essentially invisible in student Reddit discourse
FAMU president controversy shows political appointees can destabilize HBCUs
Federal funding cuts threaten HBCU admin stability nationally
key_quotes: FAMU alums blast MAGA President at Trustee meeting — accused of being an absentee leader and poor fundraiser.
She doesn't give a damn about FAMU and her political allies sure as hell don't.
Who is on financial code red? Everyone I've talked to has said their institutions are.

key_points: Dover, Delaware is a small, quiet city with limited nightlife options
DSU campus described positively by one visitor as most impressive HBCU seen
Delaware's traffic and mix of transient regional population noted as downsides
key_quotes: I am so very impressed with Delaware State!! Of all the HBCU colleges I've seen, I am most impressed with this one!!
Traffic always been bad in Delaware — people from NJ, New York, Maryland, and PA all pass through.
To outsiders it's boring, but you have to be down to know where the fun is.

key_points: No DSU-specific career placement data surfaces in student discussions
HBCU alumni networks are active and career-boosting post-graduation
Graduation rate gap vs. PWIs is a real risk to degree completion
key_quotes: As an alumni you have so many opportunities to stay connected and you build bonds with HBCU alums in the world.
I'm 1st generation to college, 1st generation law school and pretty soon, 1st generation doctor — don't worry about what those other folks got.
You wanna guess how many HBCUs have a graduation rate above 50%? Only 7.

key_points: DSU student had tuition crisis with no backup options — aid gaps are real
Smaller HBCUs like DSU often undercut Howard/Spelman on cost
MacKenzie Scott donations improving endowments but DSU not yet a recipient
key_quotes: PLEASE HELP – My tuition deadline is coming up and I'm out of options.
Apply everywhere! Tuskegee offered me a full ride. Hampton offered me a full ride.
Black elitism has too many of the parents in a chokehold — there are so many amazing institutions that will offer full rides.

key_points: Strong HBCU pride and community warmth evident in DSU football coverage
Campus described as welcoming to all backgrounds, not just Black students
DSU student online community is extremely thin — Reddit is nearly a ghost town
key_quotes: The Delaware St. Hornets has been a welcome mat for The MEAC for years.
I am so very impressed with Delaware State!! Keep up the good work!!!
My son will be an incoming freshman this year — we are excited for him.

key_points: **Campus Safety:** Teen girl murdered on campus by non-students allowed to roam freely
**Financial Aid:** Student posted emergency tuition plea with zero safety net available
**Community:** DSU's own subreddit has near-zero student engagement — serious isolation signal
key_quotes: 2 charged in teen girl's murder at Delaware State University — 'I'm sure they had no plans on enrolling in college. Why were they there?'
Time to keep all non-students off campus — we must protect our young Black students who are doing the right thing.
Yeah great school, every person mentioned here is a non-student on campus. When I think of my kid being at university, this is not what comes to mind.

key_points: DeSean Jackson coaching has genuinely revitalized DSU football program
Delaware's no-sales-tax shopping and proximity to Philly/DC is a real lifestyle perk
DSU's HBCU identity offers tight-knit community rare at larger institutions
key_quotes: DeSean Jackson has really turned that program around in a short period of time. Big ups to Coach Jack.
My husband grew up in Lewes — he loved the slower, lower lifestyle.
Our HBCUs are intimate — only two have more than 10,000 students — but the legacy is larger than life!
